#Category Data
```{r}
full_data<- read.csv("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/fullData.csv")


dx, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Q2_proteins_FinalGeneID.csv")

colnames(full_data)
```
# Long fromat data 
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
 
# ============================================
# STEP 1: PREPARE DATA WITH ALL COLUMNS
# ============================================

cat("=== Preparing aggregated dataset ===\n")

# Assuming your data structure is:
# dex columns: [other columns] | code (ID) | Time | Value (burnout) | sex | age | Protein1 | Protein2 | ... | Protein5400

# Extract components
meta_cols <- full_data[, c("code", "sex", "age", "Time", "Value","burnout", "time_numeric" )]  # Adjust column names as needed
protein_cols <- Ex  # Proteins columns use EX

# Combine
full_data <- cbind(meta_cols, protein_cols)

write.csv(full_data, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/full_dataExOriginal.csv")


# Rename for clarity
colnames(full_data)[c(1,4,6)] <- c("ID", "Timepoint", "Burnout")


cat("Original data dimensions:", dim(full_data), "\n")
cat("Columns: ID, Timepoint, Burnout, sex, age, +", ncol(protein_cols), "proteins\n\n")
```

# ============================================
# STEP 2: AGGREGATE BY ID AND TIMEPOINT
# ============================================

```{r}
# ============================================
# SIMPLEST AGGREGATION APPROACH
# ============================================

# Specify your columns explicitly
data_to_aggregate <- data.frame(
  ID = full_data$ID,
  Timepoint = full_data$Timepoint,
  Burnout = full_data$Burnout,
  sex = full_data$sex,
  age = full_data$age,
  Ex  # All protein columns
)

# Aggregate using aggregate() function
library(dplyr)

aggregated <- data_to_aggregate %>%
  group_by(ID, Timepoint) %>%
  summarise(
    Burnout = mean(Burnout, na.rm = TRUE),
    sex = first(sex),
    age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    across(where(is.numeric) & !any_of(c("Burnout", "age")), ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

write.csv(aggregated, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Aggregated_Simple.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved: Aggregated_Simple.csv\n")
cat("Dimensions:", dim(aggregated), "\n")

aggregated_data<-aggregated
```


# ============================================
# STEP 3: VERIFY AGGREGATION
# ============================================

```{r}
cat("=== Verification ===\n")

# Check observations per ID
obs_per_id <- table(table(aggregated_data$ID))
cat("Observations per ID:\n")
print(obs_per_id)
cat("\n")

# Check timepoints
cat("Timepoints present:\n")
print(table(aggregated_data$Timepoint))
cat("\n")

# Sample of aggregated data
cat("Sample of aggregated data (first 5 rows, first 8 columns):\n")
print(head(aggregated_data[, 1:min(8, ncol(aggregated_data))], 5))
cat("\n")

# ============================================
# STEP 4: SAVE AGGREGATED DATASET
# ============================================

# Save complete aggregated data
write.csv(aggregated_data, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Aggregated_Data_by_ID_Timepoint.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved: Aggregated_Data_by_ID_Timepoint.csv\n\n")
```


# ============================================
# STEP 5: CREATE SUMMARY STATISTICS
# ============================================
```{r}
cat("=== Summary Statistics ===\n")

# Summary by timepoint
summary_by_timepoint <- aggregated_data %>%
  group_by(Timepoint) %>%
  summarise(
    n_subjects = n(),
    mean_burnout = mean(Burnout, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_burnout = sd(Burnout, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_burnout = min(Burnout, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_burnout = max(Burnout, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("Summary by Timepoint:\n")
print(summary_by_timepoint)
cat("\n")

write.csv(summary_by_timepoint, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Summary_by_Timepoint.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Summary by ID
summary_by_id <- aggregated_data %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  summarise(
    n_timepoints = n(),
    mean_burnout = mean(Burnout, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_burnout = sd(Burnout, na.rm = TRUE),
    burnout_change = last(Burnout) - first(Burnout),  # T6 - T1
    sex = first(sex),
    age = first(age)
  )

cat("Summary by ID (first 10):\n")
print(head(summary_by_id, 10))
cat("\n")

write.csv(summary_by_id, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Summary_by_ID.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# ============================================
# STEP 6: CREATE WIDE FORMAT (ONE ROW PER ID)
# ============================================

cat("=== Creating wide format (one row per ID) ===\n")

library(tidyverse)

# Pivot to wide format: one row per ID, columns for each timepoint
wide_data <- aggregated_data %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(ID, sex, age),
    names_from = Timepoint,
    values_from = c(Burnout, all_of(colnames(protein_cols))),
    names_sep = "_"
  )

cat("Wide format dimensions:", dim(wide_data), "\n")
cat("Columns: ID, sex, age, + Burnout_T1-T6 + each protein at T1-T6\n\n")

write.csv(wide_data, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Wide_Format_by_ID.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved: Wide_Format_by_ID.csv\n\n")

# ============================================
# STEP 7: EXTRACT SPECIFIC FORMATS FOR ANALYSIS
# ============================================

# Format 1: Long format with only ID, Timepoint, Burnout, Proteins
long_format_simple <- aggregated_data %>%
  select(ID, Timepoint, Burnout, all_of(colnames(protein_cols)))

write.csv(long_format_simple, "Long_Format_Simple.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved: Long_Format_Simple.csv\n")
cat("  Format: ID | Timepoint | Burnout | Protein1 | ... | Protein5400\n\n")

# Format 2: Matrix format for proteins only (for correlation analysis)
protein_matrix <- as.matrix(aggregated_data[, colnames(protein_cols)])
rownames(protein_matrix) <- paste(aggregated_data$ID, 
                                  aggregated_data$Timepoint, 
                                  sep = "_")
write.csv(protein_matrix, "Protein_Matrix_Averaged.csv", row.names = TRUE)
cat("Saved: Protein_Matrix_Averaged.csv\n")
cat("  Format: Rows = ID_Timepoint, Columns = Proteins\n\n")

# ============================================
# STEP 8: VISUALIZE AGGREGATION RESULTS
# ============================================

library(ggplot2)

# Plot 1: Burnout across timepoints
p1 <- ggplot(aggregated_data, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Burnout)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", 
               shape = 23, size = 3, fill = "red") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Burnout Distribution Across Timepoints (Aggregated Data)",
    subtitle = "Red diamonds = mean",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Burnout Score"
  )

print(p1)
ggsave("Burnout_by_Timepoint_Aggregated.png", p1, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Plot 2: Individual trajectories
p2 <- ggplot(aggregated_data, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Burnout, group = ID)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3, color = "gray50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 1) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = mean, geom = "line", 
               color = "red", linewidth = 1.5) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = mean, geom = "point", 
               color = "red", size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Individual Burnout Trajectories (Aggregated Data)",
    subtitle = "Red line = mean trajectory",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Burnout Score"
  )

print(p2)
ggsave("Individual_Trajectories_Aggregated.png", p2, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Plot 3: Sample size at each timepoint
sample_size_plot <- ggplot(summary_by_timepoint, 
                           aes(x = Timepoint, y = n_subjects)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_subjects), vjust = -0.5, size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Sample Size at Each Timepoint",
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Number of Subjects"
  ) +
  ylim(0, max(summary_by_timepoint$n_subjects) * 1.15)

print(sample_size_plot)
ggsave("Sample_Size_by_Timepoint.png", sample_size_plot, 
       width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# ============================================
# STEP 9: CHECK FOR DUPLICATES/ISSUES
# ============================================

cat("=== Quality Checks ===\n")

# Check for duplicate ID-Timepoint combinations
duplicates <- aggregated_data %>%
  group_by(ID, Timepoint) %>%
  filter(n() > 1)

if(nrow(duplicates) > 0) {
  cat("WARNING: Found", nrow(duplicates), "duplicate ID-Timepoint combinations!\n")
  print(head(duplicates))
} else {
  cat("✓ No duplicate ID-Timepoint combinations\n")
}

# Check for missing values in key columns
missing_burnout <- sum(is.na(aggregated_data$Burnout))
missing_proteins <- sum(is.na(aggregated_data[, colnames(protein_cols)]))

cat("Missing values:\n")
cat("  Burnout:", missing_burnout, "\n")
cat("  Proteins:", missing_proteins, "total (", 
    round(missing_proteins / (nrow(aggregated_data) * ncol(protein_cols)) * 100, 2), 
    "%)\n\n")

# ============================================
# STEP 10: CREATE METADATA FILE
# ============================================

metadata_aggregated <- aggregated_data %>%
  select(ID, Timepoint, Burnout, sex, age, n_obs)

write.csv(metadata_aggregated, "Metadata_Aggregated.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved: Metadata_Aggregated.csv\n")

# ============================================
# SUMMARY REPORT
# ============================================

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("AGGREGATION COMPLETE\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("1. Aggregated_Data_by_ID_Timepoint.csv\n")
cat("   - Long format: ID | Timepoint | Burnout | Proteins (averaged)\n")
cat("   - Dimensions:", dim(aggregated_data), "\n\n")

cat("2. Wide_Format_by_ID.csv\n")
cat("   - Wide format: One row per ID, columns for each timepoint\n")
cat("   - Dimensions:", dim(wide_data), "\n\n")

cat("3. Long_Format_Simple.csv\n")
cat("   - Simplified: ID | Timepoint | Burnout | Proteins only\n\n")

cat("4. Protein_Matrix_Averaged.csv\n")
cat("   - Matrix format: Rows = ID_Timepoint, Columns = Proteins\n\n")

cat("5. Summary files:\n")
cat("   - Summary_by_Timepoint.csv\n")
cat("   - Summary_by_ID.csv\n")
cat("   - Metadata_Aggregated.csv\n\n")

cat("6. Visualizations:\n")
cat("   - Burnout_by_Timepoint_Aggregated.png\n")
cat("   - Individual_Trajectories_Aggregated.png\n")
cat("   - Sample_Size_by_Timepoint.png\n\n")

cat("========================================\n\n")
```

```{r}
pcomb<- p1|p2|sample_size_plot
print(pcomb)
ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/pcombTraj.png", pcomb, 
       width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)

ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/pcombTraj.pdf", pcomb, 
       width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

4-category analysis on aggregated data
# ============================================
# STEP 1: CALCULATE WITHIN-SUBJECT CHANGES
# ============================================
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(tidyr)

# ============================================
# STEP 1: CALCULATE WITHIN-SUBJECT CHANGES
# ============================================

cat("=== Calculating Within-Subject Changes ===\n\n")

# Assuming 'result' is your aggregated data from previous step
# If you named it differently, adjust accordingly
aggregated_data<-read.csv("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Aggregated_Data_by_ID_Timepoint.csv")
```

```{r}
# Calculate mean for each timepoint
means_by_timepoint <- aggregated_data %>%
  group_by(Timepoint) %>%
  summarise(
    n_subjects = n(),
    mean_burnout = mean(Burnout, na.rm = TRUE),
    across(all_of(protein_cols), ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  arrange(Timepoint)

cat("Mean values calculated for each timepoint:\n")
print(means_by_timepoint[, 1:8])  # Show first few columns
cat("\n")

# Save means
write.csv(means_by_timepoint, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Mean_Values_by_Timepoint.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


# ============================================
# STEP 2: ANALYZE BURNOUT TRAJECTORY
# ============================================

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
means_by_timepoint<- read.csv("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Olink_Cat4_Cat2/Mean_Values_by_Timepoint.csv")


cat("=== Analyzing Burnout Trajectory ===\n\n")

# Get burnout trajectory
burnout_trajectory <- means_by_timepoint %>%
  select(Timepoint, mean_burnout) %>%
  arrange(Timepoint)

cat("Burnout trajectory (T1 to T6):\n")
print(burnout_trajectory)
cat("\n")

# Calculate burnout change from T1 to T6
burnout_t1 <- burnout_trajectory$mean_burnout[burnout_trajectory$Timepoint == "C1"]
burnout_t3 <- burnout_trajectory$mean_burnout[burnout_trajectory$Timepoint == "C3"]
burnout_t6 <- burnout_trajectory$mean_burnout[burnout_trajectory$Timepoint == "C6"]

burnout_overall_change <-burnout_t6 - burnout_t1

cat("Burnout T1:", round(burnout_t1, 3), "\n")
cat("Burnout T3:", round(burnout_t3, 3), "\n")
cat("Burnout T6:", round(burnout_t6, 3), "\n")
cat("Overall change (T6-T1):", round(burnout_overall_change, 3), "\n")
cat("Direction:", ifelse(burnout_overall_change > 0, "INCREASING", "DECREASING"), "\n\n")
```


# ============================================
# STEP 3: ANALYZE PROTEIN TRAJECTORIES
# ============================================
```{r}
# ============================================
# STEP 3: ANALYZE PROTEIN TRAJECTORIES
# ============================================

cat("=== Analyzing Protein Trajectories ===\n\n")

# Function to categorize one protein based on trajectory
categorize_protein_trajectory <- function(protein_name, means_by_timepoint, burnout_trajectory) {
  
  # Get protein trajectory
  protein_values <- means_by_timepoint[[protein_name]]
  timepoints <- means_by_timepoint$Timepoint
  
  # Get T1 and T6 values
  protein_t1 <- protein_values[timepoints == "C1"]
 # protein_t2 <- protein_values[timepoints == "C2"]
 # protein_t3 <- protein_values[timepoints == "C3"]
  #protein_t4 <- protein_values[timepoints == "C4"]
 # protein_t5 <- protein_values[timepoints == "C5"]
  protein_t6 <- protein_values[timepoints == "C6"]
  
  # Calculate change if it is lasting 
  protein_change <- protein_t6 - protein_t1
  
  # Calculate correlation with burnout
  cor_result <- cor.test(burnout_trajectory$mean_burnout, protein_values)
  correlation <- cor_result$estimate
  cor_pvalue <- cor_result$p.value
  
  # Linear trend test
  time_numeric <- 1:length(timepoints)
  lm_protein <- lm(protein_values ~ time_numeric)
  protein_slope <- coef(lm_protein)[2]
  protein_trend_p <- summary(lm_protein)$coefficients[2, 4]
  
  lm_burnout <- lm(burnout_trajectory$mean_burnout ~ time_numeric)
  burnout_slope <- coef(lm_burnout)[2]
  
  # Determine category based on trajectories
  category <- "No clear pattern"
  
  # Check if changes are significant (using arbitrary but reasonable thresholds)
  protein_change_pct <- abs(protein_change) / abs(protein_t1) * 100
  
  if(protein_trend_p < 0.05) {  # Protein has significant trend
    
    if(protein_slope > 0 && burnout_slope > 0) {
      # Both increasing
      category <- "Cat1: Protein ↑ as Burnout ↑ (T1→T6)"
      
    } else if(protein_slope < 0 && burnout_slope < 0) {
      # Both decreasing
      category <- "Cat2: Protein ↓ as Burnout ↓ (T1→T6)"
      
    } else if(protein_slope < 0 && burnout_slope > 0) {
      # Protein decreasing while burnout increasing
      category <- "Cat3: Protein ↓ as Burnout ↑ (T1→T6)"
      
    } else if(protein_slope > 0 && burnout_slope < 0) {
      # Protein increasing while burnout decreasing
      category <- "Cat4: Protein ↑ as Burnout ↓ (T1→T6)"
    }
  }
  
  return(data.frame(
    protein = protein_name,
    protein_T1 = protein_t1,
    #protein_T3 = protein_t3,
    protein_T6 = protein_t6,
    protein_change = protein_change,
    protein_change_pct = protein_change_pct,
    protein_slope = protein_slope,
    protein_trend_p = protein_trend_p,
    correlation_with_burnout = correlation,
    cor_pvalue = cor_pvalue,
    category = category,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ))
}

# Analyze all proteins
cat("Analyzing trajectories for", length(protein_cols), "proteins...\n")

trajectory_results <- data.frame()

pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = length(protein_cols), style = 3)

for(i in 1:length(protein_cols)) {
  result_i <- categorize_protein_trajectory(protein_cols[i], 
                                             means_by_timepoint, 
                                             burnout_trajectory)
  trajectory_results <- rbind(trajectory_results, result_i)
  setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)
}

close(pb)

# Multiple testing correction
trajectory_results$fdr_trend <- p.adjust(trajectory_results$protein_trend_p, method = "BH")
trajectory_results$fdr_correlation <- p.adjust(trajectory_results$cor_pvalue, method = "BH")

cat("\n\n")
unique(trajectory_results$category)
colnames(trajectory_results)
write.csv(trajectory_results, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Olink_Cat4_Cat2/trajectory_results.csv")
```

# ============================================
# STEP 4: EXTRACT 4 CATEGORIES
# ============================================
```{r}
trajectory_results<- read.csv("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Olink_Cat4_Cat2/trajectory_results.csv")


cat("=== Extracting 4 Categories ===\n\n")

# Category 1: Protein increases as burnout increases
cat1_traj <- trajectory_results[
  trajectory_results$category == "Cat1: Protein ↑ as Burnout ↑ (T1→T6)" &
  trajectory_results$cor_pvalue < 0.05, ]
cat1_traj <- cat1_traj[order(-cat1_traj$protein_slope), ]

cat("CATEGORY 1: Proteins INCREASE as Burnout INCREASES (T1→T6)\n")
cat("Count:", nrow(cat1_traj), "\n")
if(nrow(cat1_traj) > 0) {
  cat("Top 10:\n")
  print(cat1_traj[1:min(10, nrow(cat1_traj)), 
                  c("protein", "protein_change", "protein_slope", "correlation_with_burnout", "protein_trend_p")])
}
cat("\n")

# Category 2: Protein decreases as burnout decreases
cat2_traj <- trajectory_results[
  trajectory_results$category == "Cat2: Protein ↓ as Burnout ↓ (T1→T6)" &
  trajectory_results$cor_pvalue < 0.05, ]
cat2_traj <- cat2_traj[order(cat2_traj$protein_slope), ]

cat("CATEGORY 2: Proteins DECREASE as Burnout DECREASES (T1→T6)\n")
cat("Count:", nrow(cat2_traj), "\n")
if(nrow(cat2_traj) > 0) {
  cat("Top 10:\n")
  print(cat2_traj[1:min(10, nrow(cat2_traj)), 
                  c("protein", "protein_change", "protein_slope", "correlation_with_burnout", "protein_trend_p")])
}
cat("\n")

# Category 3: Protein decreases as burnout increases
cat3_traj <- trajectory_results[
  trajectory_results$category == "Cat3: Protein ↓ as Burnout ↑ (T1→T6)" &
  trajectory_results$cor_pvalue < 0.05, ]
cat3_traj <- cat3_traj[order(cat3_traj$protein_slope), ]

cat("CATEGORY 3: Proteins DECREASE as Burnout INCREASES (T1→T6)\n")
cat("Count:", nrow(cat3_traj), "\n")
if(nrow(cat3_traj) > 0) {
  cat("Top 10:\n")
  print(cat3_traj[1:min(10, nrow(cat3_traj)), 
                  c("protein", "protein_change", "protein_slope", "correlation_with_burnout", "protein_trend_p")])
}
cat("\n")

# Category 4: Protein increases as burnout decreases
cat4_traj <- trajectory_results[
  trajectory_results$category == "Cat4: Protein ↑ as Burnout ↓ (T1→T6)" &
  trajectory_results$cor_pvalue < 0.05, ]
cat4_traj <- cat4_traj[order(-cat4_traj$protein_slope), ]

cat("CATEGORY 4: Proteins INCREASE as Burnout DECREASES (T1→T6)\n")
cat("Count:", nrow(cat4_traj), "\n")
if(nrow(cat4_traj) > 0) {
  cat("Top 10:\n")
  print(cat4_traj[1:min(10, nrow(cat4_traj)), 
                  c("protein", "protein_change", "protein_slope", "correlation_with_burnout", "protein_trend_p")])
}
cat("\n")
```

# ============================================
# STEP 5: SAVE RESULTS
# ============================================

```{r}
# ============================================
# STEP 5: SAVE RESULTS
# ============================================

write.csv(trajectory_results, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Trajectory_Analysis_All_Proteins.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(cat1_traj, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/Cat1_Trajectory_Protein_UP_Burnout_UP.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(cat2_traj, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/Cat2_Trajectory_Protein_DOWN_Burnout_DOWN.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(cat3_traj, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/Cat3_Trajectory_Protein_DOWN_Burnout_UP.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(cat4_traj, "/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/Cat4_Trajectory_Protein_UP_Burnout_DOWN.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("Files saved!\n\n")
```

# ============================================
# VISUALIZATION 1: BURNOUT TRAJECTORY
# ============================================
```{r}
# ============================================
# VISUALIZATION 1: BURNOUT TRAJECTORY
# ============================================

p_burnout <- ggplot(burnout_trajectory, aes(x = Timepoint, y = mean_burnout, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5, color = "black") + #D32F2F
  geom_point(size = 4, color = "black") + #D32F2F
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_burnout, 2)), vjust = -1, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  labs(
    title = "Burnout Trajectory Across Timepoints",
    subtitle = paste0("Overall change: ", round(burnout_overall_change, 3)),
    x = "Timepoint",
    y = "Mean Burnout Score"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

print(p_burnout)
ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Burnout_Trajectory_T1_to_T6.png", p_burnout, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

# ============================================
# VISUALIZATION 2: BAR CHART OF CATEGORIES
# ============================================

```{r}
# ============================================
# VISUALIZATION 2: BAR CHART OF CATEGORIES
# ============================================

summary_traj <- data.frame(
  Category = c(
    "Cat 1:\nProtein ↑\nBurnout ↑",
    "Cat 2:\nProtein ↓\nBurnout ↓",
    "Cat 3:\nProtein ↓\nBurnout ↑",
    "Cat 4:\nProtein ↑\nBurnout ↓"
  ),
  Count = c(nrow(cat1_traj), nrow(cat2_traj), nrow(cat3_traj), nrow(cat4_traj)),
  Type = c("Parallel", "Parallel", "Opposite", "Opposite")
)

p_bar <- ggplot(summary_traj, aes(x = Category, y = Count, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), vjust = -0.5, size = 8, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Parallel" = "#E53935", "Opposite" = "#1E88E5")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Protein Trajectories Relative to Burnout (T1→T6)",
    subtitle = "Based on average values at each timepoint",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of Proteins",
    fill = "Trajectory Type"
  ) +
  ylim(0, max(summary_traj$Count) * 1.2)

print(p_bar)

pTraj<- (p_burnout/p_bar)
print(pTraj)

ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/BarChart_4Categories_Trajectories.png", p_bar, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)


ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/BarChart_4Categories_Trajectories.pdf", pTraj, width = 5, height = 7, dpi = 300)

```

# ============================================
# VISUALIZATION 3: EXAMPLE TRAJECTORIES
# ============================================

```{r}
# ============================================
# VISUALIZATION 3: EXAMPLE TRAJECTORIES
# ============================================
means_data<-means_by_timepoint
burnout_traj<-burnout_trajectory
# Function to plot trajectory for one protein
plot_trajectory <- function(protein_name, means_data, burnout_trajectory, category_name) {
  
  # Prepare data
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    Timepoint = means_data$Timepoint,
    Protein = means_data[[protein_name]],
    Burnout = burnout_trajectory$mean_burnout
  )
  
  # Normalize to 0-1 scale for comparison
  plot_data$Protein_norm <- (plot_data$Protein - min(plot_data$Protein)) / 
                             (max(plot_data$Protein) - min(plot_data$Protein))
  plot_data$Burnout_norm <- (plot_data$Burnout - min(plot_data$Burnout)) / 
                             (max(plot_data$Burnout) - min(plot_data$Burnout))
  
  # Reshape for plotting
  plot_long <- plot_data %>%
    select(Timepoint, Protein_norm, Burnout_norm) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(Protein_norm, Burnout_norm),
                 names_to = "Variable",
                 values_to = "Value")
  
  p <- ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = Timepoint, y = Value, 
                              color = Variable, group = Variable)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Protein_norm" = "#2196F3", "Burnout_norm" = "#F44336"),
      labels = c("Burnout", "Protein")
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(category_name, ": ", protein_name),
      subtitle = "Normalized trajectories (0-1 scale)",
      x = "Timepoint",
      y = "Normalized Value",
      color = ""
    ) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
  
  return(p)
}

# Plot examples from each category
example_plots <- list()

if(nrow(cat1_traj) > 0) {
  example_plots[[1]] <- plot_trajectory(cat1_traj$protein[1], means_by_timepoint, 
                                         burnout_trajectory, "Category 1")
}

if(nrow(cat2_traj) > 0) {
  example_plots[[2]] <- plot_trajectory(cat2_traj$protein[1], means_by_timepoint, 
                                         burnout_trajectory, "Category 2")
}

if(nrow(cat3_traj) > 0) {
  example_plots[[3]] <- plot_trajectory(cat3_traj$protein[1], means_by_timepoint, 
                                         burnout_trajectory, "Category 3")
}

if(nrow(cat4_traj) > 0) {
  example_plots[[4]] <- plot_trajectory(cat4_traj$protein[1], means_by_timepoint, 
                                         burnout_trajectory, "Category 4")
}

# Only combine if we have plots - FIXED
if(length(example_plots) > 0) {
  # Calculate grid layout
  n_plots <- length(example_plots)
  ncol <- 2
  nrow <- ceiling(n_plots / ncol)
  
  # Save individually first
  for(i in 1:length(example_plots)) {
    ggsave(paste0("Example_Trajectory_", names(example_plots)[i], ".png"), 
           example_plots[[i]], width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
  }
  
  # Then try to combine
  tryCatch({
    p_combined <- grid.arrange(grobs = example_plots, ncol = ncol)
    ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Example_Trajectories_4Categories_Combined.png", p_combined, 
           width = 14, height = nrow * 5, dpi = 300)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Note: Combined plot failed, but individual plots saved successfully\n")
  })
}
```

# ============================================
# VISUALIZATION 4: TOP 5 TRAJECTORIES - FIXED
# ============================================

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(patchwork)

# ============================================
# FUNCTION: PLOT ALL PROTEINS IN A CATEGORY
# ============================================

plot_category_trajectories <- function(protein_list, means_data, burnout_traj, 
                                       category_name, color, n_proteins = NULL) {
  
  # Get proteins (all or specified number)
  if(is.null(n_proteins)) {
    proteins_to_plot <- protein_list$protein
  } else {
    proteins_to_plot <- head(protein_list$protein, n_proteins)
  }
  
  if(length(proteins_to_plot) == 0) {
    cat("No proteins to plot for", category_name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  cat("Plotting", length(proteins_to_plot), "proteins for", category_name, "\n")
  
  # Prepare protein data
  plot_data <- means_data %>%
    select(Timepoint, all_of(proteins_to_plot)) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(proteins_to_plot),
                 names_to = "Protein",
                 values_to = "Value") %>%
    group_by(Protein) %>%
    mutate(Value_norm = (Value - min(Value, na.rm = TRUE)) / 
                        (max(Value, na.rm = TRUE) - min(Value, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Prepare burnout data
  burnout_data <- data.frame(
    Timepoint = burnout_traj$Timepoint,
    Protein = "Burnout",
    Value_norm = (burnout_traj$mean_burnout - min(burnout_traj$mean_burnout)) /
                 (max(burnout_traj$mean_burnout) - min(burnout_traj$mean_burnout))
  )
  
  # Combine
  plot_data_combined <- rbind(
    plot_data[, c("Timepoint", "Protein", "Value_norm")], 
    burnout_data
  )
  
  # Create overlay plot (all proteins on one plot)
  p_overlay <- ggplot(plot_data_combined, 
                      aes(x = Timepoint, y = Value_norm, 
                          color = Protein, group = Protein)) +
    geom_line(aes(linewidth = Protein == "Burnout", 
                  alpha = Protein == "Burnout")) +
    geom_point(data = filter(plot_data_combined, Protein == "Burnout"),
               size = 3) +
    scale_linewidth_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 2.5, "FALSE" = 0.8), guide = "none") +
    scale_alpha_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 1, "FALSE" = 0.6), guide = "none") +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c("Burnout" = "#F44336", 
                 setNames(rep(color, length(proteins_to_plot)), proteins_to_plot)),
      breaks = "Burnout"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12)
    ) + theme_bw()+
    labs(
      title = category_name,
      subtitle = paste(length(proteins_to_plot), "proteins (normalized 0-1) + Burnout trajectory"),
      x = "Timepoint",
      y = "Normalized Expression (0-1)"
    )
  
  # Create faceted plot (individual panels for each protein)
  n_cols <- ceiling(sqrt(length(proteins_to_plot)))
  
  p_faceted <- ggplot(plot_data, 
                      aes(x = Timepoint, y = Value_norm, group = Protein)) +
    geom_line(color = color, linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(color = color, size = 2) +
    # Add burnout trajectory to each panel
    geom_line(data = burnout_data,
              aes(x = Timepoint, y = Value_norm),
              color = "#F44336", linewidth = 1.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    facet_wrap(~ Protein, ncol = n_cols, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
      strip.text = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
      panel.spacing = unit(0.3, "lines"),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(category_name, "- Individual Trajectories"),
      subtitle = "Black = Protein | Red dashed = Burnout",
      x = "Timepoint",
      y = "Normalized Expression"
    ) + theme_bw()
  
  return(list(overlay = p_overlay, faceted = p_faceted))
}

# ============================================
# PLOT CATEGORY 2 (58 PROTEINS)
# ============================================

cat("\n=== Plotting Category 2 (58 proteins) ===\n\n")

if(exists("cat2_traj") && nrow(cat2_traj) > 0) {
  
  cat2_plots <- plot_category_trajectories(
    protein_list = cat2_traj,
    means_data = means_by_timepoint,
    burnout_traj = burnout_trajectory,
    category_name = "Category 2: Protein ↓ as Burnout ↓",
    color = "#FF6F00",
    n_proteins = NULL  # Plot all proteins
  )
  
  if(!is.null(cat2_plots)) {
    
    # Save overlay plot
    print(cat2_plots$overlay)
    ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Olink_Cat4_Cat2/Cat2_Overlay_58_Proteins.png", 
           cat2_plots$overlay, 
           width = 10, 
           height = 8, 
           dpi = 300)
     ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Olink_Cat4_Cat2/Cat2_Overlay_58_Proteins.pdf", 
           cat2_plots$overlay, 
           width = 10, 
           height = 8, 
           dpi = 300)
    cat("✓ Cat2_Overlay_58_Proteins.png\n")
    
    # Save faceted plot
    print(cat2_plots$faceted)
    ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Olink_Cat4_Cat2/Cat2_Faceted_58_Proteins.png", 
           cat2_plots$faceted, 
           width = 16, 
           height = 20, 
           dpi = 300)
      ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Olink_Cat4_Cat2/Cat2_Faceted_58_Proteins.pdf", 
           cat2_plots$faceted, 
           width = 16, 
           height = 20, 
           dpi = 300)
    cat("✓ Cat2_Faceted_58_Proteins.png\n\n")
  }
  
} else {
  cat("Category 2 not found or empty\n\n")
}
```

           cat4_plots$overlay, 
           width = 10, 
           height = 8, 
           dpi = 300)
     ggsave("/Users/nasimbararpour/Library/CloudStorage/Box-Box/NasimBararpour/Lasting_Change_Study/Olink/Datasets/res_lmerlongitudinal/Olink_Cat4_Cat2/Cat4_Overlay_20_Proteins.pdf", 
           cat4_plots$overlay, 
           width = 10, 
           height = 8, 
           dpi = 300)
    cat("✓ Cat4_Overlay_20_Proteins.png\n")

           cat4_plots$faceted, 
           width = 12,

           cat4_plots$faceted,
